headline: Grammys 2025 Here's the list of performers and presenters  GMA News Online
text:
The Grammy Awards 2025 are happening this weekend, and the night’s performers and presenters just make for more excitement.
The Recording Academy announced the performers for music’s biggest night.
Namely, they are: Benson Boone, Billie Eilish, Chappell Roan, Charli XCX, Doechii, Lady Gaga and Bruno Mars, RAYE, Sabrina Carpenter, Shaboozey, Shakira, and Teddy Swims.
Those performing for the tribute segments are Brad Paisley, Brittany Howard, Coldplay’s Chris Martin, Cynthia Erivo, Herbie Hancock, Jacob Collier, Janelle Monáe, John Legend, Lainey Wilson, Sheryl Crow, St. Vincent, and Stevie Wonder.
Big names in music are also set to present the awards, ranging from rising to veteran artists. The presenters are: Taylor Swift, Olivia Rodrigo, SZA, Cardi B, Anthony Kiedis & Chad Smith, Gloria Estefan, Olivia Rodrigo, Queen Latifah, Victoria Monét, and Will Smith.
Beyonce leads the 2025 Grammy nominations with 11 for her album “Country Carter.”
Billie Eilish, Charli XCX, Kendrick Lamar, and Post Malone have seven nominations each. Taylor Swift, Chappell Roan, and Sabrina Carpenter scored six each.
The 67th Annual Grammy Awards will be held on February 2 at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. It will be hosted by Trevor Noah. —Nika Roque/JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Doc Yappy jumps on 'fine dining restaurant' trend  GMA News Online
text:
Doc Yappy amused netizens by hopping on the “fine dining restaurant” trend.
The cosmetic surgeon and Status by Sparkle talent shared a TikTok video of their family’s fancy Chinese New Year feast at the Hilton Manila. Alongside Doc Yappy were his wife Vina and their children.
For the audio, Doc Yappy used Toni Fowler’s trending “Tayo ay nasa fine dining restaurant” meme as he showed clips from their dinner.
“Parang ok lang naman pala kina Ms Vina at Doc Yappy HAHAHAHAHA,” a follower said.
“Napagalitan pa si Mikay. Okay lang naman pala kina Doc Yappy,” another fan added.
Dr. Eric C. Yapjuangco, or “Doc Yappy,” is a friend of Toni, who is also his regular client. He and his wife were mentioned in the trending audio.
In a 2024 episode of Toni's online reality show “ToRo Family,” she called out Mikay for her impolite attitude when they were dining at a fancy restaurant with Doc Yappy and Vina.
"Noong dinner, anong nangyari? May inaabot ka kay Papi. P***** ina, nalaglag 'yung biskuwit, tama? Tayo ay nasa fine dining restaurant 'di ba? So 'yung pagka-squatter natin, iwan natin sa bahay,” Toni told Mikay.
She also told Mikay to adjust to situations like that.
“Nasa restaurant ka naman na sosyal. Kailan mo 'ko narinig na 'p*****ng ina naman eh.' Naggagaganoon ako sa harap ni Ms. Vina? Never," Mommy Oni said. "Ang tawag doon, pakikisama. Hindi ka naman magiging peke eh.”
 
 
—Nika Roque/JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Scattered rain expected over eastern Luzon -PAGASA  GMA News Online
text:
The easterlies will bring cloudy skies with scattered rain and thunderstorms over the Bicol Region and Quezon province, according to PAGASA’s Saturday afternoon forecast.
Similar conditions are expected in Cagayan Valley and Aurora due to the Northeast Monsoon, which will also bring partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated light rains over Metro Manila, Ilocos Region, the Cordillera Administrative Region, and the rest of Central Luzon.
The rest of the country will have partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers or thunderstorms. 
PAGASA warns of possible flash floods or landslides in affected areas.
Moderate to strong winds with moderate to rough seas are expected over Northern and Central Luzon, while the rest of the country will experience light to moderate winds with slight to moderate seas.
Sunrise in Metro Manila on Sunday is set for 6:24 a.m. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: DOH Schools may hire nurses, health experts for sexuality education  GMA News Online
text:
The Department of Health (DOH) on Saturday highlighted that there are other ways to address teen pregnancy amid the controversy over Senate Bill 1979, or the Adolescent Pregnancy Prevention Bill.
This, as the DOH reiterated that there is no need for a new law to tackle the issue and that the Responsible Parenthood and Reproductive Health Law of 2012 (RH Law) just needs proper implementation.
One step to implementing the law properly is hiring health professionals or experts to teach the students, according to DOH Assistant Secretary Dr. Albert Domingo in an interview on Super Radyo dzBB.
“Meron kasi tayong tinatawag na MAPEH, yung Music, Arts, Physical Education and Health. Ang implementation niton ay isa lang siya sa ‘MAPEH,” said Domingo.
Instead of having only one teacher for MAPEH, Domingo said the "Health" subject must have a specific and adequately trained teacher. 
"Mas maganda kung may designated na health teacher. Isipin niyo yung pe teacher, maton na basketball coach, tapos magtuturo din siya ng reproductive health. Parang medyo awkward yung dating. Dapat yung teacher ay properly trained, Hindi lahat at kontin lang ang merong health teacher. Baka iyan ang pwedeng solusyon,” explained Domingo.
(It is better if there is a designated health teacher. Think of the PE teacher bullying the basketball coach; he will also teach reproductive health. The former seems a bit awkward. The teacher must be trained appropriately; not all, and only a few, have a health teacher. Maybe that could be the solution.)
He added that since there is still a shortage of teachers in the country, employing some nurses in schools could be a solution.
Meanwhile, Senate Deputy Minority Leader Risa Hontiveros introduced a substitute bill to Senate Bill 1979 with amendments addressing concerns about Comprehensive Sexual Education (CSE).
Hontiveros, chair of the Senate Women, Children, Family Relations, and Gender Equality Committee, expressed confidence that the revised bill would address President Ferdinand "Bongbong" Marcos Jr.'s concerns about the previous version. 
DOH assured that they would help Congress to create measures to teach comprehensive sexuality education (CSE) in schools more effectively.
—VAL, GMA Integrated News

headline: 'Everything About My Wife' unveils official poster starring Jennylyn Mercado, Dennis Trillo, Sam Milby  GMA News Online
text:
“Everything About My Wife” has released its official poster featuring lead cast Jennylyn Mercado, Dennis Trillo, and Sam Milby.
In the poster, Dennis looks alarmed as Jennylyn grabs Sam by his tie. It also bears text: “‘Pag ang better naging worse, can love heal everything?”
In January, the romantic-comedy film gave a glimpse into the married lives of couple Dom (Dennis) and Imogen (Jennylyn) through its official trailer.
Sam’s character will get in the way of the couple and will put Imogen's loyalty to the test. Imogen and Dom will try to figure out if love can still heal everything when things have turned the other way around.
According to the poster, the film will also star Carmi Martin, Alex Agustin, Joyce Glorioso, Polo Laurel, and Nova Villa, with the special participation of Ruby Ruiz, Donna Cariaga, Isay Alvarez, Romnick Sarmenta, and Atty. Annette Gozon-Valdes.
It is directed by Real Florido, written by Rona Co, and produced by GMA Pictures, CreaZion Studios, and Glimmer Studio.
"Everything About My Wife" is coming to cinemas on February 26.
 
 
—Carby Rose Basina/JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Carla Abellana is now selling handmade candles  GMA News Online
text:
Carla Abellana has delved into candle-making, and is now selling her creations for the first time.
On Instagram, the Kapuso star shared photos of her candles, which were placed in glass and ceramic containers.
“As promised, here are my newest homemade scented candles, which (for the first time ever) I’m now selling!” Carla said.
“Guys, first time ko magbenta ng candles ko so sana suportahan niyo ako dito, hehehe,” she added.
Carla sells her candles via Carousell. They have a linen cotton scent, which the actress said is similar to fresh laundry.
“I work very hard on my candles at home using only safe and premium ingredients like 100% soy wax and high-quality fragrance oils,” Carla said in her listing.
The candle in the white ceramic vessel has a wooden wick and is priced at P650. The one in the clear glass has a cotton wick and is selling for P850.
Aside from creating candles, Carla also enjoys soap making, which she finds healing and therapeutic. The actress previously announced that she would sell the soap.
Carla also opened an Instagram shop in 2024 to sell her pre-loved items.
The actress most recently starred as Georgina Palacios in “Widows’ War.”
She told GMA News Online that she plans on going to Japan for vacation after the series to unwind.
 
A post shared by Carla Abellana (@carlaangeline)
 
—Nika Roque/JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Looking for a late-night Japanese fix This Antipolo izakaya has you covered  GMA News Online
text:
Located in the heart of upper Antipolo, ABCT Izakaya & Kaitenzushi brings the authentic charm of an izakaya to the local dining scene.
The restaurant evokes the ambiance of Japanese pubs with its dimly lit interiors and wooden accents, where family and friends come together and enjoy delectable food and drinks.
According to owner Bernadette Coronado, who has been living in Japan for 17 years now, izakayas are casual pubs where workers often go after a long day to relax, eat, and drink with colleagues or friends.
 
Unlike formal dining, the izakaya way of eating is about ordering small, shareable plates paired with alcohol and enjoying a slow, social meal.
“You will start ordering drinks, and after no’n ay mga appetizers or pulutan. After no’n, ‘pag medyo lasing na, tsaka sila mag-oorder ng main dish,” she said.
Among the must-try food items in the restaurant are maguro tataki (P460), ebi tempura (P490), ebi gyoza (P260), Mt. Fuji maki (P460), potato salad (P160), teppanyaki (starts at P450), and sukiyaki (P890).
Aside from their wide variety of beers, ABCT offers fruit sour drinks (starts at P99/glass), which are shochu-based drinks that complement the rich, umami flavors of their food.
ABCT Izakaya & Kaitenzushi is located at Sakura Circle near Ynares Center, Antipolo City. —JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: GPH, MILF vow to fulfill commitments to sustain peace gains  GMA News Online
text:
The Government of the Philippines (GPH) and the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) Peace Implementing Panels pledged to fulfill their commitments under the Comprehensive Agreement on the Bangsamoro (CAB).
In a Joint Statement released by the GPH and MILF panels after their 36th meeting, both parties agreed to carry out initiatives that aim to push forward, build on, and preserve the gains of peace in the Bangsamoro.
Co-chairs Cesar B. Yano and Mohagher M. Iqbal of the GPH-MILF Peace Implementing Panel signed the statement, which emphasized the following important topics of conversation and significant agreements reached by both sides:
The meeting was held on Friday, January 31, 2025, when GPH-MILF peace panels thanked President Ferdinand "Bongbong" Marcos Jr. for his administration's steadfast commitment to the peace process in Bangsamoro.
The panels also recognized the efforts of Presidential Peace Adviser Secretary Carlito G. Galvez Jr. of the Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation, and Unity (OPAPRU) to ensure the successful implementation of the CAB.
"Based on the agreements made by both panels during the meeting, we are confident that the Bangsamoro region will continue to be a beacon of hope, peace and empowerment," Galvez said. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Small medevac plane crashes in Philadelphia with child, 5 others on board  GMA News Online
text:
A medevac plane crashed soon after takeoff in Philadelphia on Friday with a child and five others on board, the air ambulance company that operated it said, adding that it had not confirmed any survivors.
Jet Rescue Air Ambulance, based in Mexico and licensed to operate in the U.S., said its aircraft crashed with four crew members, one pediatric medical patient and the patient's mother on board.
"At this time we cannot confirm any survivors," the company said in a statement.
State and local officials said late on Friday they could not yet confirm how many people may have died on the ground after the plane slammed into a heavily populated portion of the city. Videos taken by witnesses of the crash clearly showed body parts strewn about the streets and inside nearby homes.
The Mexican government said all those on the plane were Mexican nationals, CNN reported.
The child was a girl on her way home with a final destination of Tijuana, Shai Gold, who works on corporate strategy with Jet Rescue Air Ambulance, told CNN. Her mother was also aboard, he said.
"We are terribly shocked by this tragic turn of events," Gold said. "This was a very seasoned crew. We are a leading air ambulance company, we fly 600 to 700 times a year."
He said the company had invested heavily in maintaining its aircraft to the highest international standards and that the plane that crashed had been in excellent flying condition.
"We really don't know what happened," Gold said.
Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro told a press conference at the crash scene that "we know there will be loss in this region."
"We want to offer our thoughts and our serious prayers for those that are grieving at this moment," Shapiro said.
President Donald Trump wrote on social media that it was "so sad to see the plane go down in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. More innocent souls lost. Our people are totally engaged. First Responders are already being given credit for doing a great job."
The crash follows this week's collision of an American Airlines jet and a US Army Black Hawk helicopter over Washington, D.C., which killed 67 in the deadliest airplane crash in the US since 2009.
The Federal Aviation Administration said six people were on the Learjet 55 that crashed around 6:30 p.m. (0030 GMT) on Friday. Local media reported it was near the Roosevelt Mall in northeast Philadelphia and that there were multiple injuries on the ground.
Video aired on local TV stations showed the plane in a sharp dive before hitting the ground and exploding in a massive fireball.
Philadelphia Mayor Cherelle Parker told a press conference at the scene that several houses and cars had been engulfed in flames. She said the situation is "all hands on deck, that's where we are right now."
Officials said it was not clear what led to the crash. The weather was cold and rainy and with low visibility when the plane went down.
The air ambulance had left Northeast Philadelphia Airport and was headed to Springfield-Branson National Airport in Missouri, about 1,100 miles (1,800 km) to the southwest, the FAA said in a statement.
A large fire and several fire trucks were visible at the crash scene in images broadcast by the Philadelphia CBS affiliate. About two hours after the crash the fires were mostly out, according to TV images.
The Philadelphia police and fire departments did not respond to requests for comment. —Reuters
